The legacy executor.py\nsrc/fetch/__init__.py:32:    FetchStrand,\nsrc/fetch/__init__.py:42:    \"FetchStrand\",\nsrc/agents/self_modification.py:24:# (hot-reload), matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py and\n.beads/traces/adc-6tld/stdout.txt:708:{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"call_fc38b5b6b5dc4bbd9ade45e7\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"1\\t# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\\n2\\t\\n3\\t## Overview\\n4\\t\\n5\\taide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\\n6\\t\\n7\\tCLI invocation: `adc`\\n8\\t\\n9\\t---\\n10\\t\\n11\\t## Problem Statement\\n12\\t\\n13\\tManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\\n14\\t\\n15\\taide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\\n16\\t\\n17\\t---\\n18\\t\\n19\\t## Core Architecture\\n20\\t\\n21\\t### The Hot Path\\n22\\t\\n23\\t```\\n24\\tUser utterance (voice or text)\\n25\\t  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\\n26\\t  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms)\\n27\\t      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment}, ...]\\n28\\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\\n29\\t      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\\n30\\t      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s)\\n31\\t      output: {data, summary, urgency}\\n32\\t  → Results streamed via SSE to active canvas\\n33\\t  → Cards rendered client-side using component templates\\n34\\t```\\n35\\t\\n36\\tTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\\n37\\t\\n38\\t### The Async Path\\n39\\t\\n40\\t```\\n41\\tTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\\n42\\t  → NEEDLE task bead created (br create)\\n43\\t  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\\n44\\t  → Bead watcher detects closure event\\n45\\t  → Result written to session store results table\\n46\\t  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\\n47\\t```\\n48\\t\\n49\\t### The Self-Improvement Path\\n50\\t\\n51\\t```\\n52\\tFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\\n53\\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\\n54\\t      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML, component template)\\n55\\t      generates update\\n56\\t      surfaces diff to user\\n57\\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\\n58\\t```\\n59\\t\\n60\\t---\\n61\\t\\n62\\t## Components\\n63\\t\\n64\\t### 1. Intent Router\\n65\\t\\n66\\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\\n67\\t\\n68\\tOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\\n69\\t\\n70\\t```json\\n71\\t[\\n72\\t  {\\n73\\t    \\\"project_slug\\\": \\\"options-pipeline\\\",\\n74\\t    \\\"intent_type\\\": \\\"status\\\",\\n75\\t    \\\"urgency\\\": \\\"normal\\\",\\n76\\t    \\\"utterance_fragment\\\": \\\"has the options pipeline caught up?\\\"\\n77\\t  },\\n78\\t  {\\n79\\t    \\\"project_slug\\\": \\\"ibkr-mcp\\\",\\n80\\t    \\\"intent_type\\\": \\\"status\\\",\\n81\\t    \\\"urgency\\\": \\\"normal\\\",\\n82\\t    \\\"utterance_fragment\\\": \\\"what's the state of the ibkr mcp\\\"\\n83\\t  }\\n84\\t]\\n85\\t```\\n86\\t\\n87\\tIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`\\n88\\t\\n89\\tThe router reads its segmentation prompt and the project registry from disk on each call — no caching. Hot-reload is automatic.\\n90\\t\\n91\\tAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \\\"clarification\\\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\\n92\\t\\n93\\t### 2. Project Registry\\n94\\t\\n95\\t**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\\n96\\t\\n97\\tDefines projects the router knows about:\\n98\\t\\n99\\t```yaml\\n100\\tprojects:\\n101\\t  options-pipeline:\\n102\\t    aliases: [\\\"the pipeline\\\", \\\"options\\\"]\\n103\\t    description: \\\"Options data pipeline on apexalgo-iad\\\"\\n104\\t    cluster: apexalgo-iad\\n105\\t    namespace: options\\n106\\t    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm]\\n107\\t    workflows:\\n108\\t      deploy:\\n109\\t        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, argocd-sync, pod-status]\\n110\\t\\n111\\t  ibkr-mcp:\\n112\\t    aliases: [\\\"ibkr\\\", \\\"the mcp\\\"]\\n113\\t    description: \\\"IBKR MCP server\\\"\\n114\\t    cluster: apexalgo-iad\\n115\\t    namespace: ibkr-mcp\\n116\\t    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup]\\n117\\t```\\n118\\t\\n119\\tUpdated by the self-modification agent. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\\n120\\t\\n121\\t### 3. Fetch Strand\\n122\\t\\n123\\t**Runtime:** Deterministic code, no LLM\\n124\\t\\n125\\tExecutes a command matrix based on `intent_type` and the project's registry entry. No LLM decisions — the commands are determined by the intent type and project config.\\n126\\t\\n127\\tExample command matrix for `intent_type: status`:\\n128\\t\\n129\\t| Source | Command |\\n130\\t|--------|---------|\\n131\\t| Pod status | `kubectl get pods -n {namespace}` |\\n132\\t| ArgoCD sync | `curl argocd-ro-api/applications/{app}` |\\n133\\t| Git log | `git -C {repo_path} log -10 --oneline` |\\n134\\t| Bead list | `br list --project {slug} --status open` |\\n135\\t| CI status | `kubectl get workflows -n argo-workflows -l project={slug}` |\\n136\\t\\n137\\tResults are structured data passed to the Synthesize strand. Fetch runs each source concurrently; partial results (from sources that respond first) are passed to Synthesize incrementally to support streaming output.\\n138\\t\\n139\\tA `fetch_coverage` field tracks which sources succeeded and which failed. Failed sources are surfaced as caveats in the result.\\n140\\t\\n141\\t### 4. Synthesize Strand\\n142\\t\\n143\\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\\n144\\t\\n145\\tTakes `{intent_spec, fetched_context}` → produces `{data, summary}`.\\n146\\t\\n147\\t- `data`: structured JSON matching the component library's expected schema for this intent type\\n148\\t- `summary`: 1-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode\\n149\\t\\n150\\tSingle-turn inference. Reads its system prompt from a file per invocation (hot-reload). The synthesize prompt is the highest-leverage artifact in the system — it defines result format, detail level, and what fields to include.\\n151\\t\\n152\\tRuns once per intent thread in parallel across all active threads.\\n153\\t\\n154\\t### 5. Escalate Strand\\n155\\t\\n156\\t**Runtime:** Direct API call + `br create`\\n157\\t\\n158\\tFor task-profile intents that need durable async handling:\\n159\\t1. One LLM call to formulate a bead body from the intent + context\\n160\\t2. `br create` to create the bead in the appropriate project\\n161\\t3. Returns a pending-card spec with the bead reference\\n162\\t\\n163\\tThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\\n164\\t\\n165\\t### 6. Voice Model\\n166\\t\\n167\\t**Runtime:** Realtime API (OpenAI or Claude Realtime, persistent session)\\n168\\t\\n169\\tThe conversational layer. Receives transcribed utterances (or audio directly), calls `dispatch_intent()` tool to trigger routing, and narrates results at appropriate moments.\\n170\\t\\n171\\tTool calls are triggers, not queries. `dispatch_intent()` returns an acknowledgment immediately; results arrive out-of-band and are surfaced by the voice model at natural conversational pauses.\\n172\\t\\n173\\tThe voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\\n174\\t\\n175\\tIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\\n176\\t\\n177\\t### 7. UI-Regen Agent\\n178\\t\\n179\\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\\n180\\t\\n181\\tSteward of the component library. Given a result's data shape:\\n182\\t1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions)\\n183\\t2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\\n184\\t3. Applies the component template to result data accounting for layout bucket\\n185\\t4. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\\n186\\t\\n187\\tMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\\n188\\t\\n189\\tCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\\n190\\t\\n191\\t### 8. Self-Modification Agent\\n192\\t\\n193\\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\\n194\\t\\n195\\tReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\\n196\\t- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\\n197\\t- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\\n198\\t- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, etc.)\\n199\\t- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\\n200\\t- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\\n201\\t- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\\n202\\t- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\\n203\\t\\n204\\tWorkflow for every modification:\\n205\\t1. Read current artifact\\n206\\t2. Understand intent of change\\n207\\t3. Generate updated artifact\\n208\\t4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\\n209\\t5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\\n210\\t6. On rejection: discard\\n211\\t\\n212\\tSafety model:\\n213\\t- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\\n214\\t- All artifacts versioned; any update can be rolled back with one instruction\\n215\\t- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\\n216\\t\\n217\\t### 9. Background Analysis Bead\\n218\\t\\n219\\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead, low priority)\\n220\\t\\n221\\tRuns on a schedule. Reads intent history and engagement signals from the session store, identifies patterns, proposes artifact updates.\\n222\\t\\n223\\tExamples:\\n224\\t- \\\"User consistently drills into status results for more detail → propose adding causality fields to synthesize prompt\\\"\\n225\\t- \\\"User ignores pipeline monitoring pushes for 5 days → propose lowering urgency tier\\\"\\n226\\t- \\\"After deploy status, user always asks about pod logs within 2 minutes → propose speculative pre-fetch\\\"\\n227\\t\\n228\\tProposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold.\\n229\\t\\n230\\t### 10. Bead Watcher\\n231\\t\\n232\\t**Runtime:** Daemon process, no LLM\\n233\\t\\n234\\tWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. On close:\\n235\\t1. Reads `session_id` from closed bead metadata\\n236\\t2. Looks up active surface in session store\\n237\\t3. Writes result to `results` table\\n238\\t4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\\n239\\t\\n240\\tPure I/O. Runs as a long-running process (Deployment, not a K8s Job per infrastructure conventions).\\n241\\t\\n242\\t---\\n243\\t\\n244\\t## Data Model\\n245\\t\\n246\\t### Session Store (SQLite)\\n247\\t\\n248\\t```sql\\n249\\tsessions (\\n250\\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n251\\t  created_at  INTEGER,\\n252\\t  last_active INTEGER,\\n253\\t  primary_surface_id TEXT\\n254\\t)\\n255\\t\\n256\\tsurfaces (\\n257\\t  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n258\\t  session_id      TEXT,\\n259\\t  type            TEXT,  -- 'canvas' | 'telegram' | 'audio'\\n260\\t  state           TEXT,  -- 'active' | 'idle' | 'disconnected'\\n261\\t  always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- 1 for Telegram\\n262\\t  last_seen       INTEGER\\n263\\t)\\n264\\t\\n265\\tutterances (\\n266\\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n267\\t  session_id  TEXT,\\n268\\t  raw_text    TEXT,\\n269\\t  created_at  INTEGER\\n270\\t)\\n271\\t\\n272\\tintents (\\n273\\t  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n274\\t  utterance_id TEXT,\\n275\\t  session_id   TEXT,\\n276\\t  topic_id     TEXT,\\n277\\t  project_slug TEXT,\\n278\\t  intent_type  TEXT,\\n279\\t  status       TEXT,  -- 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'resolved' | 'cancelled'\\n280\\t  bead_ref     TEXT,  -- set for task-profile intents\\n281\\t  created_at   INTEGER,\\n282\\t  resolved_at  INTEGER\\n283\\t)\\n284\\t\\n285\\tresults (\\n286\\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n287\\t  intent_id   TEXT,\\n288\\t  topic_id    TEXT,\\n289\\t  session_id  TEXT,\\n290\\t  summary     TEXT,\\n291\\t  data        TEXT,  -- JSON\\n292\\t  urgency     TEXT,  -- 'critical' | 'high' | 'normal' | 'low'\\n293\\t  created_at  INTEGER,\\n294\\t  surfaced_at INTEGER,\\n295\\t  acked_at    INTEGER\\n296\\t)\\n297\\t\\n298\\ttopics (\\n299\\t  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n300\\t  label        TEXT,\\n301\\t  type         TEXT,  -- 'project' | 'research' | 'personal' | 'exception' | 'compound'\\n302\\t  project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\\n303\\t  scope        TEXT,  -- 'session' | 'cross-session' | 'global'\\n304\\t  session_id   TEXT,  -- null for cross-session/global\\n305\\t  created_at   INTEGER,\\n306\\t  last_active  INTEGER,\\n307\\t  archived_at  INTEGER\\n308\\t)\\n309\\t\\n310\\ttopic_context_cache (\\n311\\t  topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n312\\t  context_data TEXT,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\\n313\\t  fetched_at  INTEGER,\\n314\\t  expires_at  INTEGER\\n315\\t)\\n316\\t\\n317\\tfeedback_signals (\\n318\\t  signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n319\\t  signal_type  TEXT,\\n320\\t  session_id   TEXT,\\n321\\t  result_id    TEXT,\\n322\\t  topic_id     TEXT,\\n323\\t  timestamp    INTEGER,\\n324\\t  data         TEXT,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\\n325\\t  surface_type TEXT,\\n326\\t  processed    INTEGER,  -- 0 or 1\\n327\\t  processed_at INTEGER\\n328\\t)\\n329\\t\\n330\\tintent_topics (\\n331\\t  intent_id TEXT,\\n332\\t  topic_id  TEXT,\\n333\\t  PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id)\\n334\\t)\\n335\\t```\\n336\\t\\n337\\t### Component Library (SQLite, separate DB)\\n338\\t\\n339\\t```sql\\n340\\tcomponents (\\n341\\t  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n342\\t  name          TEXT,\\n343\\t  description   TEXT,\\n344\\t  html_template TEXT,\\n345\\t  version       INTEGER,\\n346\\t  created_at    INTEGER,\\n347\\t  last_used     INTEGER,\\n348\\t  usage_count   INTEGER\\n349\\t)\\n350\\t\\n351\\tcomponent_versions (\\n352\\t  component_id  TEXT,\\n353\\t  version       INTEGER,\\n354\\t  html_template TEXT,\\n355\\t  created_at    INTEGER,\\n356\\t  change_note   TEXT,\\n357\\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, version)\\n358\\t)\\n359\\t\\n360\\tcard_cache (\\n361\\t  result_id         TEXT,\\n362\\t  component_id      TEXT,\\n363\\t  component_version INTEGER,\\n364\\t  layout_bucket     TEXT,  -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\\n365\\t  rendered_html     TEXT,\\n366\\t  created_at        INTEGER,\\n367\\t  PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\\n368\\t)\\n369\\t\\n370\\tcomponent_tags (\\n371\\t  component_id TEXT,\\n372\\t  tag TEXT,\\n373\\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, tag)\\n374\\t)\\n375\\t\\n376\\tcomponent_usage_patterns (\\n377\\t  component_id TEXT,\\n378\\t  result_type TEXT,  -- e.g., \\\"pod-status\\\", \\\"git-log\\\"\\n379\\t  match_score REAL,  -- 0-1\\n380\\t  sample_count INTEGER,\\n381\\t  last_matched INTEGER,\\n382\\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, result_type)\\n383\\t)\\n384\\t```\\n385\\t\\n386\\t---\\n387\\t\\n388\\t## File System Layout\\n389\\t\\n390\\t```\\n391\\taide-de-camp/\\n392\\t├── adc                      ← CLI entry point (shell script or Python package)\\n393\\t├── config/\\n394\\t│   ├── registry.yaml        ← project registry (hot-reloaded by router)\\n395\\t│   ├── monitoring.yaml      ← ambient monitoring rules\\n396\\t│   └── exceptions.yaml      ← exception routing rules\\n397\\t├── prompts/\\n398\\t│   ├── router.md            ← intent segmentation prompt\\n399\\t│   ├── synthesize.md        ← result generation prompt\\n400\\t│   ├── voice.md             ← voice model system prompt\\n401\\t│   ├── urgency.md           ← urgency classification prompt\\n402\\t│   ├── fetch/               ← per-intent-type fetch instructions\\n403\\t│   │   ├── status.md\\n404\\t│   │   ├── action.md\\n405\\t│   │   └── ...\\n406\\t│   └── escalate/            ← escalate/task-profile prompts\\n407\\t│       └── task-profile.md\\n408\\t├── src/\\n409\\t│   ├── main.py              ← FastAPI app entry point\\n410\\t│   ├── registry.py          ← project registry loader\\n411\\t│   ├── agents/              ← agent implementations\\n412\\t│   │   ├── self_modification.py  ← self-improvement operations\\n416\\t│   │   └── hot_reload.py        ← hot-reload watcher\\n417\\t│   ├── context/             ← context warming and pre-fetch\\n418\\t│   │   ├── warmer.py            ← context warmer for active topics\\n419\\t│   │   └── prefetch.py          ← speculative pre-fetch\\n420\\t│   ├── conversation/        ← multi-turn conversation handling\\n421\\t│   ├── diff/                ← diff generation for results\\n422\\t│   ├── environment/         ← environment and repo discovery\\n423\\t│   │   └── discovery.py        ← repo scanner and registry\\n424\\t│   ├── escalate/            ← escalate strand for task-profile intents\\n425\\t│   │   ├── handler.py           ← escalate request handler\\n426\\t│   │   ├── llm.py               ← LLM calls for bead formulation\\n427\\t│   │   └── commands.py          ├── bead creation commands\\n428\\t│   ├── feedback/            ← feedback processing and background analysis\\n429\\t│   │   ├── processor.py         ← explicit feedback processor\\n430\\t│   │   ├── signals.py           ← implicit feedback signal tracking\\n431\\t│   │   └── background_analysis.py ← background analysis bead\\n432\\t│   ├── fetch/               ← classification)\\n438\\t│   │   └── router.py            ← intent segmentation and routing\\n439\\t│   ├── memory/              ← memory store and extraction\\n440\\t│   │   ├── store.py             ← memory persistence\\n441\\t│   │   └── extraction.py        ← memory extraction from results\\n442\\t│   ├── monitoring/          ← ambient monitoring\\n443\\t│   │   └── ambient.py           ← ambient monitoring rules\\n444\\t│   ├── realtime/            ← OpenAI Realtime API voice session\\n445\\t│   │   ├── session.py           ← voice session handler\\n446\\t│   │   ├── batching.py          ← result batching for narration\\n447\\t│   │   ├── continuity.py        ← audio-to-canvas session continuity\\n448\\t│   │   └── dispatch.py          ← tool-as-trigger dispatch\\n449\\t│   ├── session/             ← session store (SQLite)\\n450\\t│   │   └── store.py             ← session store operations\\n451\\t│   ├── sse/                 ← SSE broadcasting\\n452\\t│   │   ├──  ← result surface routing logic\\n456\\t│   ├── synthesize/          ← synthesize strand (LLM)\\n457\\t│   │   └── strand.py            ← result synthesis\\n458\\t│   ├── telegram/            ←  ├── commands.py          ← CLI commands\\n469\\t│       ├── config.py            ← CLI configuration\\n470\\t│       └── sse.py               ← SSE streaming for CLI\\n471\\t├── data/\\n472\\t│   ├── session.db           ← session store (SQLite)\\n473\\t│   └── components.db        ← component library (SQLite)\\n474\\t└── docs/\\n475\\t    ├── research/            ← architecture and design research\\n476\\t    ├── plan/\\n477\\t    │   └── plan.md          ← this file\\n478\\t    └── notes/\\n479\\t        └── naming.md\\n480\\t```\\n481\\t\\n482\\t---\\n483\\t\\n484\\t## Deployment Model\\n485\\t\\n486\\taide-de-camp is a **live web application**, not a static site. The FastAPI server is the intelligence layer — it handles SSE connections, maintains session state, runs the router and synthesize strands, and serves the frontend HTML. There is no CDN path (no CF Pages).\\n487\\t\\n488\\t### Why not static\\n489\\t\\n490\\t- SSE connections are long-lived; CDNs cannot proxy them\\n491\\t- WebSocket for the Realtime API voice session requires a persistent backend\\n492\\t- The session store (SQLite) and artifact store (prompts, registry) require a writable filesystem\\n493\\t- Hot-reload depends on the running server reading updated files from disk on each invocation\\n494\\t\\n495\\t### Current Deployment: Phase 0 (Hetzner server directly)\\n496\\t\\n497\\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\\n498\\t\\n499\\tThe server runs as a process on the Hetzner server itself, not in k8s:\\n500\\t- NEEDLE workers and the aide-de-camp server share the same filesystem\\n501\\t- Self-modification agent writes directly to `prompts/` and `config/` — hot-reload works without any coordination\\n502\\t- Exposed via Tailscale (the server is already on the mesh); no ingress config needed\\n503\\t- SQLite DBs are local files; no PVC required\\n504\\t\\n505\\tRunning command: `uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`\\n506\\t\\n507\\tNo container, no CI, no ArgoCD. Managed as a long-running process (see CLAUDE.md for restart commands).\\n508\\t\\n509\\t### Release flow (Phase 0)\\n510\\t\\n511\\tVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\\n512\\t\\n513\\tRelease: `bump version in pyproject.toml` → `commit` → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → `push`.\\n514\\t\\n515\\t### Future: Phase 1+ (containerized, ardenone-cluster)\\n516\\t\\n517\\t**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\\n518\\t\\n519\\tOnce session persistence and multi-surface routing are needed, containerize and move to k8s:\\n520\\t\\n521\\t```\\n522\\tDocker image: ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp\\n523\\tDeployment: ardenone-cluster, namespace: aide-de-camp\\n524\\tPVC: /data/ (SATA, ReadWriteOnce)\\n525\\t  /data/session.db        ← session store\\n526\\t  /data/components.db     ← component library\\n527\\t  /data/prompts/          ← hot-reloadable prompt files\\n528\\t  /data/config/           ← registry.yaml, monitoring.yaml, exceptions.yaml\\n529\\t```\\n530\\t\\n531\\tThe artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from.\\n532\\t\\n533\\t### Future: Traefik configuration for SSE and WebSocket (Phase 1+)\\n534\\t\\n535\\t**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\\n536\\t\\n537\\tTraefik's default response buffering breaks SSE. The IngressRoute needs:\\n538\\t\\n539\\t```yaml\\n540\\t# IngressRoute annotations\\n541\\ttraefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: aide-de-camp-strip-prefix@kubernetescrd\\n542\\t\\n543\\t# Middleware: disable buffering, extend timeouts\\n544\\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\\n545\\tkind: Middleware\\n546\\tmetadata:\\n547\\t  name: aide-de-camp-streaming\\n548\\tspec:\\n549\\t  buffering:\\n550\\t    maxResponseBodyBytes: 0   # disable buffering\\n551\\t  headers:\\n552\\t    customResponseHeaders:\\n553\\t      X-Accel-Buffering: \\\"no\\\"\\n554\\t```\\n555\\t\\n556\\tServersTransport timeout:\\n557\\t\\n558\\t```yaml\\n559\\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\\n560\\tkind: ServersTransport\\n561\\tmetadata:\\n562\\t  name: aide-de-camp-longlived\\n563\\tspec:\\n564\\t  forwardingTimeouts:\\n565\\t    responseHeaderTimeout: 0    # no timeout on SSE streams\\n566\\t    dialTimeout: 5s\\n567\\t```\\n568\\t\\n569\\tDUCK-E's existing WebSocket IngressRoute config is the reference implementation for this cluster.\\n570\\t\\n571\\t### SQLite concurrency\\n572\\t\\n573\\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\\n574\\t\\n575\\tMultiple writers (bead watcher, background analysis bead, session handler) require WAL mode to avoid lock contention:\\n576\\t\\n577\\t```python\\n578\\t# On DB open\\n579\\tconn.execute(\\\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\\\")\\n580\\tconn.execute(\\\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\\\")\\n581\\t```\\n582\\t\\n583\\tWAL mode allows concurrent reads during writes and serializes multiple writers without blocking. For a personal single-user app, this is sufficient.\\n584\\t\\n585\\t### adc CLI connectivity\\n586\\t\\n587\\tThe `adc` CLI is a thin HTTP client. It talks to the running server over Tailscale:\\n588\\t\\n589\\t```bash\\n590\\t# ~/.config/adc/config\\n591\\tserver_url = \\\"http://localhost:8000\\\"   # Phase 0 (local)\\n592\\t# Future: \\\"http://aide-de-camp.ardenone.com\\\" (Phase 1+)\\n593\\t```\\n594\\t\\n595\\tNo local","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false,"persistedOutputPath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-aide-de-camp/8ff45f8a-f343-455f-851f-b63b1dca2f40/tool-results/b2wvs4r7g.txt","persistedOutputSize":1620012}}

All actual execution was delegated to the canonical `FetchStrand` from `orchestrator.py`:\\n\\n```python\\nasync def execute(self, command: FetchCommand) -> FetchResult:\\n    canonical_request = command.to_canonical()\\n    result = await self._strand.fetch(canonical_request)  # Delegates to canonical\\n    return FetchResult.from_canonical(...)\\n```\\n\\nNo fetch logic was implemented in `executor.py`—it only translated between the old API and the canonical implementation.\\n\\n## Canonical Implementation: `commands.py` + `orchestrator.py`\\n\\n**Purpose:** Unified fetch framework with intent-based command matrix\\n\\n### `commands.py`\\n\\n**Purpose:** Command matrix defining what to fetch per intent type\\n\\n**Key Components:**\\n\\n1. **`IntentType` enum** (8 types):\\n   - ArgoCD: `ARGOCD_APP`\\n   - Git: `GIT_LOG`, `GIT_STATUS`\\n   - Beads: `BEAD_LIST`, `BEAD_DETAILS`\\n   - CI: `CI_STATUS`\\n   - Components: `COMPONENTS`\\n   - Operations: `LOGS`, `EVENTS`\\n   - State: `SESSION_STATE`, `TOPIC_CONTEXT`, `REMINDERS`\\n   - Filesystem: `FS_EXPLORE`, `FS_README`, `FS_HOME`\\n\\n3. **`FetchCommandSpec` dataclass:**\\n   - `source`: FetchSource\\n   - `command_template`: str (e.g., `\\\"kubectl --server={proxy} get pods -n {namespace} -o json\\\"`)\\n   - `timeout_seconds`: int (default 5)\\n   - `required`: bool (default False)\\n   - `cacheable`: bool (default True)\\n\\n4. **`FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX`:**\\n   - Maps `IntentType` → `list[FetchCommandSpec]`\\n   - Defines which sources to query for each intent type\\n   - Configurable per-source timeouts\\n   - Marks required vs. optional sources\\n\\n5. **`FetchContext` dataclass:**\\n   - Context variables for template expansion\\n   - Includes `ssh_target`, `host_alias` for remote execution\\n   - Template expansion via `expand_template()` method\\n\\n6. **Result types:**\\n   - `SourceResult`: Single source result (status, data, error, duration_ms, cached)\\n   - `FetchCoverage`: Coverage report (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\\n   - `FetchResult`: Complete result with all sources and coverage\\n\\n### `orchestrator.py`\\n\\n**Purpose:** Concurrent fetch execution with streaming support\\n\\n**Key Components:**\\n\\n1. **`FetchStrand` class:**\\n   - `_source_executors`: Dict mapping `FetchSource` → async executor method\\n   - `fetch()` method:\\n     - Executes all sources concurrently via `asyncio.create_task()`\\n     - Enforces per-source timeouts via `asyncio.wait_for()`\\n     - Calls `on_partial_result` callback as each source completes\\n     - Tracks coverage (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\\n     - Generates caveats for failed sources\\n   - **17 source executor methods**: `_fetch_kubectl_pods`, `_fetch_git_log`, etc.\\n   - SSH remote execution support via `_make_cmd()` wrapper\\n\\n2. **`FetchOrchestrator` class:**\\n   - Convenience wrapper around `FetchStrand`\\n   - Provides `execute_fetch()` method with same interface\\n\\n3. **Global instances:**\\n   - `get_orchestrator()`: Returns singleton `FetchOrchestrator`\\n   - `get_fetch_strand()`: Returns singleton `FetchStrand`\\n\\n**Capabilities:**\\n- ✅ Streaming callbacks via `on_partial_result(source, result)`\\n- ✅ Concurrent execution via asyncio\\n- ✅ Per-source timeouts\\n- ✅ Comprehensive coverage tracking\\n- ✅ Caveat generation for failed sources\\n- ✅ SSH remote execution for git/filesystem operations\\n- ✅ Async HTTP client (httpx) for Kubernetes API calls\\n\\n## Capability Matrix\\n\\n| Capability | Canonical (orchestrator.py) | Deleted (strand.py) | Deleted (executor.py) |\\n|------------|----------------------------|--------------------|----------------------|\\n| Concurrent fetch execution | ✅ Native | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\\n| Streaming callbacks | ✅ `on_partial_result` | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\\n| Per-source timeouts | ✅ `timeout_seconds` in spec | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\\n| Coverage tracking | ✅ `FetchCoverage` dataclass | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\\n| Caveat generation | ✅ Automatic | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\\n| SSH remote execution | ✅ `_make_cmd()` wrapper | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\\n| Kubernetes API (async) | ✅ Httpx async client | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\\n| Git operations | ✅ Local + SSH remote | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\\n| Filesystem operations | ✅ Local + SSH remote | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\\n| Intent-based routing | ✅ `FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX` | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\\n| Source executors (17) | ✅ All implemented | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Consolidation Details\\n\\n### Commit: `34beb3c` (2026-07-03)\\n\\n**Title:** \\\"refactor: consolidate dual fetch implementations (adc-wa9)\\\"\\n\\n**Changes:**\\n1. Deleted `src/fetch/executor.py` (217 lines)\\n2. Deleted `src/fetch/strand.py` (13 lines)\\n3. Updated `src/context/warmer.py` → use `get_fetch_strand()` from orchestrator\\n4. Updated `src/context/prefetch.py` → use `get_fetch_strand()` from orchestrator\\n5. Updated `src/monitoring/ambient.py` → use `get_fetch_strand()` from orchestrator\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria (from commit):**\\n- ✅ Single fetch implementation using orchestrator+commands\\n- ✅ No module imports fetch.strand or fetch.executor\\n- ✅ Existing tests still pass\\n- ✅ CLAUDE.md and README.md already agree on fetch module names\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Key Findings\\n\\n### 1. `strand.py` Was a Minimal Shim\\n\\n**Purpose:** Allow legacy imports of `KUBECTL_PROXIES` to continue working\\n\\n**Impact:** Zero—no functionality lost, only a constant re-export\\n\\n### 2. `executor.py` Was an Adapter Layer\\n\\n**Purpose:** Translate old API (`FetchType`, `FetchCommand`) to new API (`FetchSource`, `FetchRequest`)\\n\\n**Impact:** Zero—all execution delegated to canonical `FetchStrand.fetch()`\\n\\n**Design Pattern:**\\n- `FetchType` enum → mapped to `FetchSource`\\n- `FetchCommand` → converted to `FetchRequest`\\n- `FetchResult` → converted from `SourceResult`\\n- `FetchExecutor.execute()` → delegated to `FetchStrand.fetch()`\\n\\n### 3. All Capabilities Were Already in Canonical Stack\\n\\nThe canonical implementation (`commands.py` + `orchestrator.py`) already had:\\n\\n- ✅ All 17 source executors\\n- ✅ Streaming callbacks via `on_partial_result`\\n- ✅ Coverage tracking (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\\n- ✅ Caveat generation for failed sources\\n- ✅ SSH remote execution support\\n- ✅ Per-source timeout enforcement\\n- ✅ Intent-based command matrix\\n- ✅ Required/optional source flags\\n- ✅ Cacheable flags (for future caching layer)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Migration Status\\n\\n**No migration required**—the deleted modules provided no capabilities beyond what exists in the canonical stack.\\n\\n### What Was Deleted\\n\\n1. **`strand.py`** (13 lines):\\n   - Re-exported `KUBECTL_PROXIES` constant\\n   - Existed solely for backward compatibility\\n\\n2. **`executor.py`** (217 lines):\\n   - Adapter layer translating old API to new API\\n   - Delegated all execution to canonical `FetchStrand`\\n   - No independent fetch logic\\n\\n### What Remains\\n\\n1. **`commands.py`**:\\n   - Intent types, fetch sources, command matrix\\n   - Result types and context structures\\n\\n2. **`orchestrator.py`**:\\n   - `FetchStrand` with all source executors\\n   - `FetchOrchestrator` convenience wrapper\\n   - Global instance management\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n- ✅ Comparison report exists documenting all capabilities from deleted modules\\n- ✅ List of features that need migration: **NONE**—all capabilities already present in canonical stack\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Conclusion\\n\\n**The canonical stack (`commands.py` + `orchestrator.py`) was already the complete implementation** before consolidation.
